channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.nintendolife.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.nintendolife.com

item.title: Poll: So, Will You Be Playing Final Fantasy XIV Online On Switch 2?
item.content:encoded:
Go on, tell us

After ongoing discussions about bringing the popular MMORPG Final Fantasy XIV Online to Nintendo's platform, it's finally been revealed at this week's Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival.

Final Fantasy XIV Online will be arriving in August 2026 on the Switch 2. As we've already noted, players will be able to get started with one-month period of early access. After this, you'll need to purchase a separate subscription for this version of the game.

If you already have a subscription on another platform, the Switch 2 subscription can be purchased at half-price. It's also been confirmed a Switch Online subscription won't be required to play on Switch 2. You can find out more about this version in the announcement post and Square Enix's website.

So, will you be giving Final Fantasy XIV Online a go on Switch 2? Vote in our poll and let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/poll-so-will-you-be-playing-final-fantasy-xiv-online-on-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Looks Like 'Mina The Hollower' Will Be Getting A New Release Date Soon
item.content:encoded:
The mouse has gone gold

Yacht Club Games has been pretty tight-lipped about its upcoming Game Boy-inspired, Zelda-style adventure Mina the Hollower, which is due to launch in spring 2026.

But the developer has revealed today that the game has finally gone Gold. So while we're approaching the end of that spring 2026 window, it sounds like we'll be getting a date very soon.

We would assume May, of course, but don't be surprised if it slips past that spring window, as Yacht Club Games has just "begun the submissions process with our first-party partners."

Still, we're eagerly anticipating to play this long-awaited follow-up to Shovel Knight, one of the best retro-inspired games we've ever played.

The beautiful pixel art action adventure is coming to both Switch 1 and Switch 2, with the latter supporting 120fps gameplay,, and last year, we got a limited-time demo on both consoles, which gave us a sweet early taste of the game.

The full release isn't far away, then. And we hope for the best, as Yacht Club revealed that this game could be a "make-or-break" situation for the team late last year.

Are you looking forward to Mina the Hollower? Do you think the game will be coming out soon? Let us know down below.

Alana has been with Nintendo Life since 2022, and while RPGs are her first love, Nintendo is a close second. She enjoys nothing more than overthinking battle strategies, characters, and stories. She also wishes she was a Sega air pirate.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/looks-like-mina-the-hollower-will-be-getting-a-new-release-date-soon
